```{r orginal krig chunk}
# k <- krige(depth~1, all_sources, grd, v.m)
```
Try multivariate approach- did not work
```{r topography-multivariate}
#extract topographic values for each input data location
combin_norm

plot(curve5)

extract(dem)
#flow accumulation/drainage area
mask_dem_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/w3_dems/1mdem_w3_masked.tif")
fill_output <- "./w3_dems/10mdem_fill.tif"



w3_uaa <- 
  extract(rast(flowacc_output), combin_norm, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>% 
  rename("uaa" = `X10mdem_flowacc`) #%>% 
  #as_tibble()

w3_tpi <- extract(rast(tpi_output), combin_norm, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>% 
  rename("tpi" = `X10mdem_tpi`) %>% 
  #as_tibble() %>% 
  select(tpi)

w3_twi <- extract(rast(twi_output), combin_norm, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>% 
  rename("twi" = `X10mdem_twi`) %>% 
  #as_tibble() %>% 
  select(twi)

w3_slope <- extract(rast(slope_output), combin_norm, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>% 
  rename("slope" = `X10mdem_slope`) %>% 
  #as_tibble() %>% 
  select(slope)

w3_topo <- bind_spat_cols(w3_uaa, w3_twi, w3_tpi, w3_slope)

all_sources2 <- st_as_sf(w3_topo)
which(st_is_empty(all_sources2))
all_sources2 <- all_sources2[-which(st_is_empty(all_sources2)),]
# Remove missing depth values, all 3 are in auger dataset
which(is.na(all_sources2))
all_sources2 <-all_sources2[-which(is.na(all_sources2)),]

no_auger <- filter(all_sources2, source != "2019_auger")

# Multivariate kriging
hscat(log(depth) ~ 1, no_auger, (0:9) * 100)

 cok.maps <- predict(vm.fit, meuse.grid)
# calculate the sample variogram
v <- variogram(depth~1, no_auger)
#v <- variogram(depth~1, all_sources, cutoff = 445.8491, width = 37.15409)

# plot semivariogram
plot(v, plot.numbers = TRUE, xlab = "distance h [m]",
     ylab = expression(gamma(h)),
     xlim = c(0, 1.055 * max(v$dist)))

# fit a model/variogram to the data
v.m <- fit.variogram(v, vgm(1, "Exp", 10, 1))
plot(v, v.m, plot.numbers = TRUE, xlab = "distance h [m]",
     ylab = expression(gamma(h)),
     xlim = c(0, 1.055 * max(v$dist))) ## draws a single model; draw 2 models in single plot:
# use variogram model to krig
k <- krige(depth~1, all_sources, grd, v.m)
```
```{r figuring-out-VSURF}
#p_load(VSURF)
vsurfSpam <- VSURF(point_valuespk ~ ., all, parallel = TRUE, ncores = 3, clusterType = "FORK", ntree = 500)
vsurfSpam
summary(vsurfSpam)
plot(vsurfSpam)
colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.interp])
colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.pred])

inputs <- all_df[colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.interp])]



vsurfSpamPred <- VSURF_pred(point_valuespk ~  ., all, nmj = 15, err.interp = vsurfSpam$err.interp, varselect.interp = vsurfSpam$varselect.interp)
colnames(all[vsurfSpamPred$varselect.pred])
plot(vsurfSpamPred)
all[vsurfSpamPred$varselect.pred]

varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = TRUE, main = "Variable importance")
partialPlot(vsurfSpamPred, pred.data = as.data.frame(all), x.var = "point_valuespk", main = "point_valuespk")

#no training split
all_df <- as.data.frame(all)

iris.learn <- sample(1:nrow(all_df), nrow(all_df))
iris.vsurf <- VSURF(x = all_df[,-1], y = all_df[, 1], na.action = na.omit)
plot(iris.vsurf)
colnames(all_df[,-1][iris.vsurf$varselect.interp])
colnames(all_df[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])


iris.predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all_df[, -1], step = "pred")
#calculate RMSE
tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = all_df[, 1]) %>% 
  mutate(diff = actual - iris.predictions) %>% 
  summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - iris.predictions)^2)))




#training split
iris.learn <- sample(1:nrow(all_df), nrow(all_df)*0.7)
iris.vsurf <- VSURF(all_df[iris.learn, -1], all_df[iris.learn, 1],
                            step = "pred", na.action = na.omit)
iris.predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all_df[-iris.learn, -1])
#calculate RMSE
tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = all_df[-iris.learn, 1]) %>% 
  summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2)))


# Use selected variables from VSURF to train RF using RF package
# then plot in partial dependence plot
colnames(all_df[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])

all_df <- all %>% 
  select(point_valuespk, colnames(all_df[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred]))
RFDef <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, na.action = na.omit)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

RFDefImp <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, importance = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, n.var = ncol(all) - 1, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")

#plot of partial dependence
imp <- importance(RFDefImp)
impvar <- rownames(imp)[order(imp[, 1], decreasing=TRUE)]
#impvar1 <- impvar[1:10]
#impvar1 <- colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.pred])

op <- par(mfrow=c(1, 5))
for (i in seq_along(impvar)) {
    partialPlot(RFDefImp, as.data.frame(all_df), impvar[i], xlab=impvar[i],
                #main=paste("Partial Dependence on", impvar1[i]),
                main = "",
                cex = 0.1,
                rug = TRUE,
                ylim = c(0, 1))
}
par(op)
```

Not really useful- soil pit calibration sites
```{r soil pit calibration sites}
all_cali <- read_csv("all_cali.csv")
model <- lm(log(all_cali$'max depth')~log(all_cali$h_v_max))

summary(model)

plot(all_cali$'max depth', all_cali$h_v_max)
#plot showing all calibration sites
all_cali %>% 
  dplyr::select(h_v_max, 'max depth', type, quality) %>% 
  rename(depth = 'max depth') %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  filter(depth != 12) %>% 
  filter(type == "soil pit") %>% 
ggplot()+
  geom_point(aes(x = h_v_max, y =  depth, color = type))+
  theme_classic()+
  labs(title = "Soil calibration sites",
       x = "Peak Resonance Frequency (Hz)",
       y = "Depth to bedrock (m)")
  # scale_x_continuous(trans='log2')+
  # scale_y_continuous(trans='log2')

#calculate what the shear wave velocity should be
cali$Vs <- cali$freq * 4 * cali$depth
summary(cali$Vs)
mean_Vs <- unname(summary(cali$Vs)[4])


lm_eqn <- function(m){
    eq <- substitute(italic(y) == a + b %.% italic(x)*","~~italic(r)^2~"="~r2, 
         list(a = format(unname(coef(m)[1]), digits = 2),
              b = format(unname(coef(m)[2]), digits = 2),
             r2 = format(summary(m)$r.squared, digits = 3)))
    as.character(as.expression(eq));
}

#plot showing only well calibration sites
all_cali %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = log(h_v_max), y = log('max depth')))+
  geom_point()#+
  #geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=log(avg-sd), xmax=log(avg+sd)))+
  geom_abline(intercept = model$coefficients[1],slope = model$coefficients[2],
              linetype="dashed")+
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = well))+
  theme_classic()+
  labs(title = "Just well calibration sites",
       x = "Ln of Peak Resonance Frequency (Hz)",
       y = "Ln Depth to bedrock (m)") + 
  geom_text(x = 3.5, y = 2.4, label = lm_eqn(model), parse = TRUE, size = 4)
```




RF example from textbook
```{r}
p_load(randomForest, kernlab)
# set up spam data set
data("spam", package = "kernlab")
set.seed(9146301) 
levels(spam$type) <- c("ok", "spam")
yTable <- table(spam$type)
indApp <- c(sample(1:yTable[2], yTable[2]/2), sample((yTable[2] + 1):nrow(spam), yTable[1]/2))
spamApp <- spam[indApp, ]
spamTest <- spam[-indApp, ]

# run RF on spam dataset
RFDef <- randomForest(type ~ ., data = spamApp)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

RFDoTrace <- randomForest(type ~ ., data = spamApp, ntree = 50, do.trace = 25)

RFDefImp <- randomForest(type ~ ., data = spamApp, importance = TRUE)
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, n.var = ncol(spamApp) - 1, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")
```

Try predicting straight up depth, but also depth classes
```{r}
all_df <- as.data.frame(all)
RFDef <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, na.action = na.omit)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

RFDefImp <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all, importance = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, n.var = ncol(all) - 1, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = TRUE, n.var = 10, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")

#plot of partial dependence
imp <- importance(RFDefImp)
impvar <- rownames(imp)[order(imp[, 1], decreasing=TRUE)]
impvar1 <- impvar[1:10]
#impvar1 <- colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.pred])

op <- par(mfrow=c(2, 5))
for (i in seq_along(impvar1)) {
    partialPlot(RFDefImp, all_df, impvar1[i], xlab=impvar1[i],
                #main=paste("Partial Dependence on", impvar1[i]),
                main = "",
                cex = 0.1)
}
par(op)
```

```{r figuring-out-VSURF}
#p_load(VSURF)
vsurfSpam <- VSURF(point_valuespk ~ ., all, parallel = TRUE, ncores = 3, clusterType = "FORK", ntree = 500)
vsurfSpam
summary(vsurfSpam)
plot(vsurfSpam)
colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.interp])
colnames(all[,-1][vsurfSpam$varselect.pred])

inputs <- all_df[colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.interp])]



vsurfSpamPred <- VSURF_pred(point_valuespk ~  ., all, nmj = 15, err.interp = vsurfSpam$err.interp, varselect.interp = vsurfSpam$varselect.interp)
colnames(all[vsurfSpamPred$varselect.pred])
plot(vsurfSpamPred)
all[vsurfSpamPred$varselect.pred]

varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = TRUE, main = "Variable importance")
partialPlot(vsurfSpamPred, pred.data = as.data.frame(all), x.var = "point_valuespk", main = "point_valuespk")

#no training split
all_df <- as.data.frame(all) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-point_valuesID)

iris.learn <- list()
iris.learn$training <- all_df[,-1]
iris.learn$pk <- all_df[,1]

iris.vsurf <- VSURF(x = iris.learn$training, y = iris.learn$pk, na.action = na.omit, ntree = 500, clusterType = "FORK")
plot(iris.vsurf)
colnames(iris.learn$training[iris.vsurf$varselect.interp])
colnames(iris.learn$training[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])


iris.predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = iris.learn$training, step = "pred")
#calculate RMSE
tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = iris.learn$pk) %>% 
  mutate(diff = actual - iris.predictions) %>% 
  summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - iris.predictions)^2)))




#training split
iris.learn <- sample(1:nrow(all_df), nrow(all_df)*0.7)
iris.vsurf <- VSURF(all_df[iris.learn, -1], all_df[iris.learn, 1],
                            step = "pred", na.action = na.omit)
iris.predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all_df[-iris.learn, -1])
#calculate RMSE
tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = all_df[-iris.learn, 1]) %>% 
  summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2)))


# Use selected variables from VSURF to train RF using RF package
# then plot in partial dependence plot
chosen_vars <- colnames(iris.learn$training[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])

all_df <- all %>% 
  dplyr::select(c(point_valuespk, chosen_vars))
RFDef <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, na.action = na.omit)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

RFDefImp <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, importance = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, n.var = ncol(all) - 1, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")

#plot of partial dependence
imp <- importance(RFDefImp)
impvar <- rownames(imp)[order(imp[, 1], decreasing=TRUE)]
#impvar1 <- impvar[1:10]
#impvar1 <- colnames(all[vsurfSpam$varselect.pred])

op <- par(mfrow=c(1, 5))
for (i in seq_along(impvar)) {
    partialPlot(RFDefImp, as.data.frame(all_df), impvar[i], xlab=impvar[i],
                #main=paste("Partial Dependence on", impvar1[i]),
                main = "",
                cex = 0.1,
                rug = TRUE,
                ylim = c(0, 1))
}
par(op)
```

```{r map-of-RF-error}

rf_error <- tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = iris.learn$pk) %>% 
  mutate(diff = actual - iris.predictions) %>% 
  rename(pk = actual) %>% 
  left_join(as_tibble(w3_stic_locs_r))

rf_error_vect <- vect("mod_bed_points.shp") %>% 
  mutate(ID = FID + 1) %>% 
  left_join(rf_error, by = "ID")

rf_error %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_point(aes(x = pk,y = diff))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey")+
  theme_classic()

ggplot()+
  theme_void()+
  geom_spatraster(data = hill)+
  theme_void()+
  #theme(legend.position = "")+
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("gray9", "gray48","lightgray", "white"),
                       na.value = "transparent",
                       guide = "none")+
    new_scale_fill() +
  #theme(legend.position = "")+
  geom_spatraster(data = k_terra, 
                  aes(fill = var1.pred_var1.pred),
                  alpha = 0.7)+
    scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = "transparent", name = "Depth (m)",option = "magma")+
  geom_sf(data = as.contour(dem1, nlevels = 30), color = "white", alpha = 0.1)+
      geom_sf(data = w3_regime, colour = "lightgray") +

  new_scale_fill()+
geom_sf(data = rf_error_vect, aes(fill = diff), size = 3, pch = 21) +
  scale_fill_gradient2()+
  ggspatial::annotation_scale(location = 'tr',
                              pad_x = unit(0.4, "cm"),
                              pad_y = unit(1, "cm"))+
    guides(colour = guide_coloursteps(show.limits = TRUE))
```

```{r residuals-analysis}
p_load(plotmo)

all_df <- all %>% 
  dplyr::select(c(point_valuespk, chosen_vars))

RFDef <- randomForest(point_valuespk ~ ., data = all_df, na.action = na.omit)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

plotres(RFDef, which = 3)
plotres(RFDef, which = 4)

```

```{r plot-ALE}
# ALE plot
    p_load(iml, yaImpute)
    predictor <- Predictor$new(model = RFDef, data = all_df, y = "point_valuespk")

effs <- FeatureEffects$new(predictor, grid.size = 10)
plot(effs)

predictors <- colnames(all[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])
pred.bike = Predictor$new(RFDef, data = all_df, y = "point_valuespk")
ale_interact = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, 
                                 feature = c(chosen_vars[1], chosen_vars[2]),
                                 method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot() #+
  scale_fill_gradient("ALE", low = "red", high = "yellow") + 
  scale_x_continuous("Relative Humidity") + 
  scale_y_continuous("Temperature")+
  #scale_fill_viridis(option = "D") + 
  theme(legend.position = "top")

  
  
# trying to make figure like from textbook of interacting ALE plot

pred.bike = Predictor$new(RFDef, data = all_df, y = "point_valuespk")
limits = c(-0.25, 0.25)

ale3 = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, predictors[1], method = "ale")$plot() +
  scale_x_continuous("Wind speed") +
  scale_y_continuous("ALE of bike rentals", limits=limits)

alecat1 = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, predictors[2], method = "ale") 


ale3 

ale_uaa = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = chosen_vars[3], method = "ale")$results
ale_uaa$feature = "uaa"
ale_uaa$x = ale_uaa$uaa
ale_uaa$uaa = NULL
ale_dep = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = predictors[2], method = "ale")$results
ale_dep$feature = "dep"
ale_dep$x = ale_dep$dep
ale_dep$dep= NULL

#ale_interact = 
par(mfrow=c(5, 5))
# 1
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(chosen_vars[1], chosen_vars[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(chosen_vars[1], chosen_vars[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+ 
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(chosen_vars[2], chosen_vars[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
#2
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+ 
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
#3
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+ 
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
#4
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+ 
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
#5
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+ 
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()#+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()

```

```{r 2d-ALE-grid}
#plot all in a grid
# 1
ab = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
ac = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
    #limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
ad = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
    #limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
ae = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
    #limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
#2
ba = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
bc = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
bd = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
be = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
#3
ca = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
cb = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25)) 
cd = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
ce = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[3], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
#4
da = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
db = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25)) 
dc = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
de = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[4], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
#5
ea = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[1]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))
eb = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[2]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25)) 
ec = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))#+
ed = FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[5], predictors[4]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(-0.25, 0.25))



(plot_spacer() + ab + ac + ad + ae +
 ba + plot_spacer() + bc + bd + be +
 ca + cb + plot_spacer() + cd + ce +
 da + db + dc + plot_spacer() + de +
 ea + eb + ec + ed + plot_spacer()) +
 plot_layout(ncol = 5, nrow = 5)

FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[1], predictors[5]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
FeatureEffect$new(pred.bike, feature = c(predictors[2], predictors[3]), method = "ale", grid.size = 40)$plot()+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
```


## Run RF again, but to predict baseflow permanence
```{r}
baseflow_pk

all_bflow <- all %>% rename(ID = point_valuesID) %>% 
  inner_join(baseflow_pk) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-ID, -point_valuespk) %>% 
  relocate(baseflow_pk)

#check for NAs
View(all_bflow[!complete.cases(all_bflow), ])
na_counts <- colSums(is.na(all_bflow))

names(na_counts[na_counts > 0])

#no training split
all_df_b <- as.data.frame(all_bflow)

iris.learn <- list()
iris.learn$training <- all_df_b[,-1]
iris.learn$pk <- all_df_b[,1]

iris.vsurf <- VSURF(x = iris.learn$training, y = iris.learn$pk)
plot(iris.vsurf)
colnames(all_df_b[,-1])
colnames(iris.learn$training[iris.vsurf$varselect.interp])
colnames(iris.learn$training[iris.vsurf$varselect.pred])

iris.predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all_df_b[, -1], step = "pred")
#calculate RMSE
tibble(iris.predictions, "actual" = all_df_b[, 1]) %>% 
  mutate(diff = actual - iris.predictions) %>% 
  summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean((actual - iris.predictions)^2)))

RFDef <- randomForest(baseflow_pk ~ ., data = all_df_b, na.action = na.omit)
RFDef
plot(RFDef)

RFDefImp <- randomForest(baseflow_pk ~ ., data = all_df_b, importance = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = FALSE, n.var = ncol(all) - 1, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")
varImpPlot(RFDefImp, type = 1, scale = TRUE, n.var = 10, cex = 0.8, main = "Variable importance")

data("toys")
```


# Monte Carlo experiments
## Enter the casino
GPT error propagation method
```{r}
# train power law model
# first, remove everything with sd greater than 1/2 measurement, causing problems later on
train <- cali
fit <- lm(log(depth) ~ log(freq), data = train)
summary(fit)

beta_hat <- coef(fit)        # coefficients
V_beta   <- vcov(fit)        # covariance matrix
sigma_res <- sigma(fit)      # residual SD


newdata <- 
  seis_f0 %>% 
  filter(sd < h_v_max/2) %>% 
  rename(freq = "h_v_max",
         freq_sd = "sd")
#newdata$freq
#newdata$freq_sd

nsim <- 5000   # usually 2000–10000 is good

beta_sim <- mvrnorm(nsim, beta_hat, V_beta)

simulate_depth <- function(freq, freq_sd) {

  # 1. simulate true frequencies
  freq_sim <- rnorm(nsim, mean = freq, sd = freq_sd)

  # avoid impossible values
  freq_sim[freq_sim <= 0] <- NA

  log_freq_sim <- log(freq_sim)

  # 2. regression prediction
  log_depth_sim <-
    beta_sim[,1] +
    beta_sim[,2] * log_freq_sim

  # 3. add residual variability (optional but recommended)
  log_depth_sim <-
    log_depth_sim + rnorm(nsim, 0, sigma_res)

  # 4. convert back to depth
  depth_sim <- exp(log_depth_sim)

  depth_sim
}

results <- lapply(
  seq_len(nrow(newdata)),
  function(i)
    simulate_depth(
      newdata$freq[i],
      newdata$freq_sd[i]
    )
)



newdata$depth_mean <- sapply(results, mean, na.rm=TRUE)
newdata$depth_sd   <- sapply(results, sd, na.rm=TRUE)
newdata$depth_cv <- newdata$depth_sd / newdata$depth_mean

newdata$depth_p025  <- sapply(results, quantile, 0.025, na.rm=TRUE)
newdata$depth_p50  <- sapply(results, quantile, 0.50, na.rm=TRUE) # same thing as median
newdata$depth_p975  <- sapply(results, quantile, 0.975, na.rm=TRUE)
head(newdata)
summary(newdata)
hist(results[[5]], breaks = 50,
     main = "Depth uncertainty distribution",
     xlab = "Depth")


no_crazys <- newdata %>% 
  filter(depth_sd <= 5)
hist(no_crazys$depth_p50)
summary(no_crazys)
newdata %>% 
  filter(depth_sd >= 5)

```
Second attempt
```{r}
# Assume you have calibration data:
# f0_calib (Hz), H_calib (m)
train <- cali
fit <- lm(log(depth) ~ log(freq), data = train)

df <- data.frame(
  logf0 = log(cali$freq),
  logH  = log(cali$depth)
)

fit <- lm(logH ~ logf0, data = df)

# Extract parameters
theta_hat <- coef(fit)              # (alpha, beta)
Sigma_theta <- vcov(fit)            # covariance matrix
sigma_reg <- summary(fit)$sigma     # residual std dev

newdata <- 
  seis_f0 %>% 
  filter(sd < h_v_max/2) %>% 
  rename(freq = "h_v_max",
         freq_sd = "sd")

newdata <- 
  seis_f0 %>% 
  filter(sd < 2) %>% 
  rename(freq = "h_v_max",
         freq_sd = "sd")

mc_depth <- function(f0_mean, f0_sd, n = 10000,
                     theta_hat, Sigma_theta, sigma_reg) {

  # Convert to log space
  mu_logf0 <- log(f0_mean)
  sd_logf0 <- f0_sd / f0_mean   # delta-method approximation

  # Sample f0 uncertainty
  logf0_sim <- rnorm(n, mu_logf0, sd_logf0)

  # Sample regression parameters
  theta_sim <- mvrnorm(n, mu = theta_hat, Sigma = Sigma_theta)
  alpha_sim <- theta_sim[,1]
  beta_sim  <- theta_sim[,2]

  # Sample residual noise
  eps_sim <- rnorm(n, 0, sigma_reg)

  # Compute log depth
  logH_sim <- alpha_sim + beta_sim * logf0_sim + eps_sim

  # Convert back
  H_sim <- exp(logH_sim)

  return(H_sim)
}


# Example vectors
f0_mean_vec <- newdata$freq   # mean f0 per site
f0_sd_vec   <- newdata$freq_sd   # sd of f0 per site

results <- lapply(seq_along(f0_mean_vec), function(i) {
  H_sim <- mc_depth(
    f0_mean = f0_mean_vec[i],
    f0_sd   = f0_sd_vec[i],
    n = 10000,
    theta_hat = theta_hat,
    Sigma_theta = Sigma_theta,
    sigma_reg = sigma_reg
  )

  c(
    median = median(H_sim),
    mean   = mean(H_sim),
    sd     = sd(H_sim),
    p16    = quantile(H_sim, 0.16),
    p84    = quantile(H_sim, 0.84),
    p2.5   = quantile(H_sim, 0.025),
    p97.5  = quantile(H_sim, 0.975)
  )
})

results_df <- do.call(bind_rows, results) %>% tibble()
results_df
mc_list  # list of numeric vectors (each = H_sim for a site)
length(mc_list) == number_of_sites

# depth probability density
df_plot <- data.frame(H = results_df)
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = H)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 80,
                 fill = "grey70",
                 color = "black",
                 linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(H_sim),
             linetype = "dashed",
             linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Depth to Bedrock (m)",
    y = "Probability Density"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

results_df$site <- seq_len(nrow(results_df))

ggplot(results_df,
       aes(x = site, y = median)) +

  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p2.5, ymax = p97.5),
              fill = "grey85") +

  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = p16, ymax = p84),
              fill = "grey65") +

  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +

  labs(
    x = "HVSR Site",
    y = "Depth to Bedrock (m)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)
```
```{r depth and sd}

## Plot of just seismic for AGU25 poster
seis_alone <- 
  seis_f0 %>% 
  filter(grilla_number == 20) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4329) %>% 
  st_transform(crs1) 
  #group_by(lat, long) %>%       
  #remove duplicate locations by taking the mean
  summarise(h_v_mean = mean(h_v_max), n_coords = n()) %>% # n() is for looking duplicate number
  arrange(desc(n_coords)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4329) %>% 
  st_transform(crs1)

ggplot()+
  theme_void()+
  geom_sf(data = w3_outline, color = "grey", fill = NA, alpha = 0.3, lwd = 2)+
  geom_sf(data = w3_stream, colour = "#9AC0CD", lwd = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = seis_alone, aes(fill = h_v_max), size = 2, pch = 21) +
  scale_fill_continuous(high = "purple4", low = "white",
                      name = "F0 (m/s)")+
  theme_classic()+
  theme(rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "left")

#seis_alone_sd <- 
  seis_f0 %>% 
  filter(sd < 2 * h_v_max) #%>% 
group_by(lat, long) %>%       
  #remove duplicate locations by taking the mean
  summarise(sd_mean = mean(sd), n_coords = n()) %>% # n() is for looking duplicate number
  arrange(desc(n_coords)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4329) %>% 
  st_transform(crs1) 
ggplot()+
  theme_void()+
  geom_sf(data = w3_outline, color = "grey", fill = NA, alpha = 0.3, lwd = 2)+
  geom_sf(data = w3_stream, colour = "#9AC0CD", lwd = 1) +
  geom_sf(data = seis_alone_sd, aes(fill = sd_mean), size = 2, pch = 21) +
  scale_fill_continuous(high = "red4", low = "white",
                      name = "sd")+
  theme_classic()+
  theme(rect = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "left")
ggsave("w3_map_dpeth.png" )
```

Copy and pasted from JP's github repo
```{r Bedrock weighted area}
#raster of BOSS output, downloaded from google drive of lat weathering project
library(qgisprocess)
library(RSAGA)
options(qgisprocess.path = "/Applications/QGIS.app/Contents/MacOS/qgis_process")
qgis_configure()


# install.packages("RSAGA", dependencies = TRUE)
# install.packages("qgisprocess")
library(qgisprocess)
plot(rast("bdr100k.tif"))

out_dir <- "./bedrock/"

dem <- rast(fill5_output)
weights <- rast("bdr100k.tif")

qgis_run_algorithm(algorithm = "sagang:catchmentarearecursive",
                   FLOW_UNIT = 0,
                   ELEVATION = dem,
                   METHOD = 3,
                   NO_NEGATIVES = 1,
                   ACCU_TARGET = dem,
                   FLOW = paste0(out_dir, "catcharea")
)

catcharea <- rast(paste0(out_dir, "catcharea.sdat"))
crs(catcharea) <- epsg_orig
writeRaster(catcharea, paste0(out_dir, "catcharea.tif"), overwrite = TRUE)

#weighted
#qgis_show_help("saga:catchmentarearecursive")
qgis_run_algorithm(algorithm = "sagang:catchmentarearecursive",
                   ELEVATION = dem,
                   FLOW_UNIT = 0,
                   METHOD = 3,
                   WEIGHTS = weights,
                   NO_NEGATIVES = 1,
                   ACCU_TARGET = dem,
                   FLOW = paste0(out_dir, "catcharea_br")
)

plot(rast("uaab_norm2_SHRINKED.tif"))
uaab <- (rast("uaab_norm2_SHRINKED.tif"))

crop_uaab <- crop(uaab, w3_outline)
mask_uaab <- mask(crop_uaab, w3_outline)
plot(mask_uaab)
```



Backup plan- use kmeans clustering
```{r cluster test}
# k means clustering
clusters <- no_crazys %>% 
  dplyr::select(depth_mean, depth_sd) %>% 
  kmeans(., 3, nstart = 25)

no_crazys$class <- clusters$cluster

```


# prediction of the whole network based on RF
Predicting other watersheds and the whole of W3 will be a back up if topmodel doesn't end up working as we intend
## Test on one tributary- W2
```{r generate-points-along-network}
equally_spaced_points <- st_sample(st_as_sf(W2), size = 100, type = "regular")
plot(equally_spaced_points)
# now all I have to do is extract values from the depth-DEM and I can plot again
bedrock_topo <- rast(bedrock_topo_location)
bedrock_surface <- terra::extract(bedrock_topo, vect(equally_spaced_points), xy = TRUE, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>% as_tibble()
```

Successfully used RF model on new obs! Who knows if they are accurate
```{r RF-applied-one trib}
# input points should be in form of spat vector, with EPSG:26919
input_points <- as_tibble(st_coordinates(equally_spaced_points)) %>% 
  mutate(ID = row_number()) %>% 
  vect(geom = c("X", "Y"), crs = crs1) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-L1)
writeVector(input_points, "input_pts.shp", overwrite = TRUE)
### Extracting point values ####
extract(rast(flowacc10_output), 
                  input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE)
point_values <- 
  extract(rast(slope10_output), input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope5_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope3_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble()
point_values_comb <- point_values %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("point_values", .x))
  
### extract by neighborhoods ####
# create different sized buffers around points, then conduct zonal on them
buffer10 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 10)
buffer100 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 100)

buffed_10m <- bind_cols( 
  zonal(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer10, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  as_tibble()

buffed_10m_comb <- buffed_10m %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("nb10m_", .x))

### extract by watersheds ####
d8 <- paste0(arsenal, "/w3_dems/1mdem_w3_d8pt.tif")
subsheds <- "subsheds2.tif"
wbt_watershed(d8_pntr = flowdir_location,
              pour_pts = "input_pts.shp",
              output = subsheds)
plot(rast(subsheds), col = my_colors)
zones <- rast(subsheds)
# Zonal function- take zonal statistics
# evelation
dem_r  <- resample(project(depth1, zones), zones, method="bilinear")
z_dem <- zonal(dem_r, zones, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

 
 
### extract by neighbors in watersheds SETUP ####
# delineate watersheds for each point...

#creating zone in shed function
# input_dataset <- depth10
buffer_dataset <- buffer10
plot(buffer10)
zone_in_shed <- function(input_dataset, buffer_dataset, col_name){
for(i in 1:length(buffer_dataset$ID)){
#i <- 29
#input_dataset <- rast(flowacc10_output)
#buffer_dataset <- buffer1
#col_name <- "wsnb100_depth10"
  ID <- i
  zones <- rast(subsheds)

  zones[zones != ID] <- NA
  zones_masked <- mask(zones, buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID])
  plot(zones)
  plot(buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID], add = TRUE)
  input_resamp  <- resample(project(input_dataset, zones_masked), 
                            zones_masked, method="bilinear")
  output <- zonal(input_resamp, zones_masked, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)
  if(i == 1) alldat <- output
  if(i > 1) alldat <- rbind(alldat, output)
}
  #alldat <- tibble(alldat[,2])
  colnames(alldat) <- c("ID", col_name)
  return(alldat)
}


#### Buffer 100 ####
wsnb100 <- 
  zone_in_shed(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_flowacc10") %>% 
    full_join(zone_in_shed(rast(tpi5_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_tpi5")) %>%
    full_join(zone_in_shed(curve1_plan, buffer100, "wsnb100_curve1_plan"))
#### Combine all ####

all <-
  bind_cols(
    buffed_10m_comb,
    wsnb100 %>% tibble() %>% dplyr::select(-ID),
    point_values_comb %>% dplyr::select(-c(point_valuesL1, point_valuespoint_ID))
  )


#check for NAs
View(all[!complete.cases(all), ])
na_counts <- colSums(is.na(all))

new_predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all, step = "pred")

input_points$persistence <- new_predictions
ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = input_points, aes(color = persistence))
```
## Apply to whole W3 network
Now do for the whole network!
```{r setup-W3}
w3_regime
equally_spaced_points <- st_sample(st_as_sf(w3_regime), size = 400, type = "regular")
plot(equally_spaced_points)

```
```{r RF-applied-allW3}
##### input points #####
# input points should be in form of spat vector, with EPSG:26919
input_points <- as_tibble(st_coordinates(equally_spaced_points)) %>% 
  mutate(ID = row_number()) %>% 
  vect(geom = c("X", "Y"), crs = crs1) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-L1)
writeVector(input_points, "input_pts.shp", overwrite = TRUE)
### Extracting point values ####
extract(rast(flowacc10_output), 
                  input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE)
point_values <- 
  extract(rast(slope10_output), input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope5_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope3_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble()
point_values_comb <- point_values %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("point_values", .x))
  
### extract by neighborhoods ####
# create different sized buffers around points, then conduct zonal on them
buffer10 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 10)
buffer100 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 100)

buffed_10m <- bind_cols( 
  zonal(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer10, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  as_tibble()

buffed_10m_comb <- buffed_10m %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("nb10m_", .x))

### extract by watersheds ####
d8 <- paste0(arsenal, "/w3_dems/1mdem_w3_d8pt.tif")
subsheds <- "subsheds2.tif"
wbt_watershed(d8_pntr = flowdir_location,
              pour_pts = "input_pts.shp",
              output = subsheds)
plot(rast(subsheds), col = my_colors)
zones <- rast(subsheds)
# Zonal function- take zonal statistics
# evelation
dem_r  <- resample(project(depth1, zones), zones, method="bilinear")
z_dem <- zonal(dem_r, zones, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

 
 
### extract by neighbors in watersheds SETUP ####
# delineate watersheds for each point...

#creating zone in shed function
# input_dataset <- depth10
buffer_dataset <- buffer10
plot(buffer10)
zone_in_shed <- function(input_dataset, buffer_dataset, col_name){
for(i in 1:length(buffer_dataset$ID)){
#i <- 29
#input_dataset <- rast(flowacc10_output)
#buffer_dataset <- buffer1
#col_name <- "wsnb100_depth10"
  ID <- i
  zones <- rast(subsheds)

  zones[zones != ID] <- NA
  zones_masked <- mask(zones, buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID])
  plot(zones)
  plot(buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID], add = TRUE)
  input_resamp  <- resample(project(input_dataset, zones_masked), 
                            zones_masked, method="bilinear")
  output <- zonal(input_resamp, zones_masked, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)
  if(i == 1) alldat <- output
  if(i > 1) alldat <- rbind(alldat, output)
}
  #alldat <- tibble(alldat[,2])
  colnames(alldat) <- c("ID", col_name)
  return(alldat)
}


#### Buffer 100 ####
wsnb100 <- 
  zone_in_shed(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_flowacc10") %>% 
    full_join(zone_in_shed(rast(tpi5_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_tpi5")) %>%
    full_join(zone_in_shed(curve1_plan, buffer100, "wsnb100_curve1_plan"))
#### Combine all ####

all <-
  bind_cols(
    buffed_10m_comb,
    wsnb100 %>% tibble() %>% dplyr::select(-ID),
    point_values_comb %>% dplyr::select(-point_valuesID)
  )


#check for NAs
View(all[!complete.cases(all), ])
na_counts <- colSums(is.na(all))

new_predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all, step = "pred")

input_points$persistence <- new_predictions
ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = input_points, aes(color = persistence))+
  scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Predicted Persistence")+
  theme_void()
```

## Apply to other two watersheds
In the original generation of all of the topo metrics to test, I make a bunch of different raster datasets. Will need to make them for other 2 watersheds.
```{r generate-topo-files}
# outline of shed
fb_shed_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/fb_shed.tif")
fb_outline <- as.polygons(rast(fb_shed_location), extent=FALSE)
plot(fb_outline)

##### Prepare DEMS #####
dem_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/HB/1m hydro enforced DEM/dem1m.tif")
dem1 <- rast(dem_location) %>% 
  crop(fb_outline) %>% 
  mask(fb_outline)
dem3 <- aggregate(rast(dem_location), 3) %>% 
  crop(fb_outline) %>% 
  mask(fb_outline)
dem5 <- aggregate(rast(dem_location), 5) %>% 
  crop(fb_outline) %>% 
  mask(fb_outline)
dem10 <- aggregate(rast(dem_location), 10) %>% 
  crop(fb_outline) %>% 
  mask(fb_outline)

#save raster, because whitebox wants it is a files location instead of an object in R
dem1_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/1mdem.tif")
dem3_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/3mdem.tif")
dem5_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/5mdem.tif")
dem10_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/10mdem.tif")

writeRaster(dem1, dem1_location, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(dem3, dem3_location, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(dem5, dem5_location, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(dem10, dem10_location, overwrite = TRUE)

##### STIC locations #####
stic_locs_snap_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_stic_locs.shp")
pks_fb <- read_csv(paste0(arsenal, "/pks_fb.csv"))

fb_stic_locs_r <- vect(stic_locs_snap_location)

fb_stic_location <- "fb_stic_locs.shp"
#writeVector(w3_stic_locs_r, w3_stic_location)
# come up with a set of functions to extract relevant topography and subsurface neighborhoods
# checking that DEMs are correct location, and different resolutions
plot(rast(dem1_location))
plot(rast(dem3_location))
plot(rast(dem5_location))
plot(rast(dem10_location))
### fill DEMs #####
fill1_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_fill.tif")
fill3_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_fill.tif")
fill5_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_fill.tif")
fill10_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_fill.tif")

wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = dem1_location,
  output = fill1_output
)
wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = dem3_location,
  output = fill3_output
)
wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = dem5_location,
  output = fill5_output
)
wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = dem10_location,
  output = fill10_output
)
### Drainage area ####
flowacc1_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_flowacc.tif")
flowacc3_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_flowacc.tif")
flowacc5_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_flowacc.tif")
flowacc10_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_flowacc.tif")
wbt_d_inf_flow_accumulation(input = fill1_output,
                            output = flowacc1_output,
                            out_type = "Specific Contributing Area")
wbt_d_inf_flow_accumulation(input = fill3_output,
                            output = flowacc3_output,
                            out_type = "Specific Contributing Area")
wbt_d_inf_flow_accumulation(input = fill5_output,
                            output = flowacc5_output,
                            out_type = "Specific Contributing Area")
wbt_d_inf_flow_accumulation(input = fill10_output,
                            output = flowacc10_output,
                            out_type = "Specific Contributing Area")
### Slope ####
slope1_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_slope.tif")
slope3_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_slope.tif")
slope5_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_slope.tif")
slope10_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_slope.tif")

wbt_slope(dem = fill1_output,
          output = slope1_output,
          units = "degrees")
wbt_slope(dem = fill3_output,
          output = slope3_output,
          units = "degrees")
wbt_slope(dem = fill5_output,
          output = slope5_output,
          units = "degrees")
wbt_slope(dem = fill10_output,
          output = slope10_output,
          units = "degrees")
### TPI ####
tpi1_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_tpi.tif")
tpi3_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_tpi.tif")
tpi5_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_tpi.tif")
tpi10_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_tpi.tif")

# diff resolutions, but the same kernel size
wbt_relative_topographic_position(
    dem = fill1_output, 
    output = tpi1_output, 
    filterx=11, 
    filtery=11)
wbt_relative_topographic_position(
    dem = fill3_output, 
    output = tpi3_output, 
    filterx=11, 
    filtery=11)
wbt_relative_topographic_position(
    dem = fill5_output, 
    output = tpi5_output, 
    filterx=11, 
    filtery=11)
wbt_relative_topographic_position(
    dem = fill10_output, 
    output = tpi10_output, 
    filterx=11, 
    filtery=11)
### TWI ####
twi1_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_twi.tif")
twi3_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_twi.tif")
twi5_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_twi.tif")
twi10_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_twi.tif")

wbt_wetness_index(sca = flowacc1_output, #flow accumulation
                  slope = slope1_output,
                  output = twi1_output)
wbt_wetness_index(sca = flowacc3_output, #flow accumulation
                  slope = slope3_output,
                  output = twi3_output)
wbt_wetness_index(sca = flowacc5_output, #flow accumulation
                  slope = slope5_output,
                  output = twi5_output)
wbt_wetness_index(sca = flowacc10_output, #flow accumulation
                  slope = slope10_output,
                  output = twi10_output)
### Curve ####
#1m
curve1_plan_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_curve_plan.tif")
curve1_prof_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/1mdem_curve_prof.tif")
curve1_plan <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem1_location), type = "planform")
curve1_prof <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem1_location), type = "profile")
writeRaster(curve1_plan, curve1_plan_output, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(curve1_prof, curve1_prof_output, overwrite = TRUE)
#3m 
curve3_plan_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_curve_plan.tif")
curve3_prof_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/3mdem_curve_prof.tif")
curve3_plan <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem3_location), type = "planform")
curve3_prof <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem3_location), type = "profile")
writeRaster(curve3_plan, curve3_plan_output, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(curve3_prof, curve3_prof_output, overwrite = TRUE)
#5m
curve5_plan_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_curve_plan.tif")
curve5_prof_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/5mdem_curve_prof.tif")
curve5_plan <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem5_location), type = "planform")
curve5_prof <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem5_location), type = "profile")
writeRaster(curve5_plan, curve5_plan_output, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(curve5_prof, curve5_prof_output, overwrite = TRUE)
#10m
curve10_plan_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_curve_plan.tif")
curve10_prof_output <- paste0(arsenal,"/fb_dems/10mdem_curve_prof.tif")
curve10_plan <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem10_location), type = "planform")
curve10_prof <- spatialEco::curvature(rast(dem10_location), type = "profile")
writeRaster(curve10_plan, curve10_plan_output, overwrite = TRUE)
writeRaster(curve10_prof, curve10_prof_output, overwrite = TRUE)

```
```{r}
##### input points #####
# input points should be in form of spat vector, with EPSG:26919
input_points <- fb_stic_locs_r
writeVector(input_points, "input_pts.shp", overwrite = TRUE)
### Extracting point values ####
extract(rast(flowacc10_output), 
                  input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE)
point_values <- 
  extract(rast(slope10_output), input_points, ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope5_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  extract(rast(slope3_output), ., ID = FALSE, bind = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble()
point_values_comb <- point_values %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("point_values", .x))
  
### extract by neighborhoods ####
# create different sized buffers around points, then conduct zonal on them
buffer10 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 10)
buffer100 <- terra::buffer(input_points, width = 100)

buffed_10m <- bind_cols( 
  zonal(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer10, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  as_tibble()

buffed_10m_comb <- buffed_10m %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("nb10m_", .x))

### extract by watersheds ####
# snap points to stream network used to delineate subsheds
# crop by watershed of each tributary
# read in pour points
fill1_output

flowacc1_output

flowdir_location <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/1mdem_fb_flowdir.tif")
wbt_d8_pointer(dem = fill1_output,
               output = flowdir_location)

surface_streams <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/surface_streams_1m.tif")
wbt_extract_streams(flow_accum = flowacc1_output,
                    output = surface_streams,
                    threshold = 2000)
plot(rast(surface_streams), col ="black", add = TRUE)
points(input_points, col = "red")

#snap pour point to streams
subshed_locs_snap <- "subshed_locs_snap_fb.shp"
wbt_jenson_snap_pour_points(pour_pts = "input_pts.shp",
                            streams = surface_streams,
                            output = subshed_locs_snap,
                            snap_dist = 30)

points(vect(subshed_locs_snap), col= "red")

# delineate sheds, for zonal stats
d8 <- paste0(arsenal, "/fb_dems/1mdem_fb_d8pt.tif")
plot(rast(d8))
subsheds <- "subsheds2.tif"
wbt_watershed(d8_pntr = d8,
              pour_pts = subshed_locs_snap,
              output = subsheds)
plot(rast(subsheds), col = my_colors)
zones <- rast(subsheds)
# Zonal function- take zonal statistics
# evelation
dem_r  <- resample(project(depth1, zones), zones, method="bilinear")
z_dem <- zonal(dem_r, zones, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

 
 
### extract by neighbors in watersheds SETUP ####
# delineate watersheds for each point...

#creating zone in shed function
# input_dataset <- depth10
buffer_dataset <- buffer100
plot(buffer10)
zone_in_shed <- function(input_dataset, buffer_dataset, col_name){
for(i in 1:length(buffer_dataset$ID)){
#i <- 29
#input_dataset <- rast(flowacc10_output)
#buffer_dataset <- buffer1
#col_name <- "wsnb100_depth10"
  ID <- buffer_dataset$ID[i]
  zones <- rast(subsheds)

  zones[zones != ID] <- NA
  zones_masked <- mask(zones, buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID])
  plot(zones)
  plot(buffer_dataset[buffer_dataset$ID == ID], add = TRUE)
  input_resamp  <- resample(project(input_dataset, zones_masked), 
                            zones_masked, method="bilinear")
  output <- zonal(input_resamp, zones_masked, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)
  if(i == 1) alldat <- output
  if(i > 1) alldat <- rbind(alldat, output)
}
  #alldat <- tibble(alldat[,2])
  colnames(alldat) <- c("ID", col_name)
  return(alldat)
}


#### Buffer 100 ####
wsnb100 <- 
  zone_in_shed(rast(flowacc10_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_flowacc10") %>% 
    full_join(zone_in_shed(rast(tpi5_output), buffer100, "wsnb100_tpi5")) %>%
    full_join(zone_in_shed(curve1_plan, buffer100, "wsnb100_curve1_plan"))
#### Combine all ####

# A PROBLEM TO FIGURE OUT LATER <- ONE OF THESE IS NOT YIELDING THE SAME NUMBER OF ROWS AS THE OTHERS
all <- 
  bind_cols(
    buffed_10m_comb,
    point_values_comb %>% dplyr::select(point_valuesID,
                                        point_valuesX10mdem_slope,
                                        point_valuesX5mdem_slope,
                                        point_valuesX3mdem_slope)
  ) %>% 
  rename(ID = point_valuesID) %>% 
  right_join(wsnb100, by = "ID") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-ID)

#check for NAs
View(all[!complete.cases(all), ])
na_counts <- colSums(is.na(all))

new_predictions <- predict(iris.vsurf, newdata = all, step = "pred")

input_points$persistence <- new_predictions
ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = input_points, aes(color = persistence))+
  scale_color_viridis_c(name = "Predicted Persistence")+
  theme_void()
```


# RF based on topography for all 3 watersheds
